title: Implement critical domain and governance guards

sources.reason: Defines Task 4 critical-domain and governance guard scope and acceptance.
Declares critical domains and non-capabilities that guards must enforce.
Existing Trust signal integration point for Preflight.
Existing readiness gate and machine-readable report flow.

riskAssessment.reason: Guards must fail closed for critical domains, governance bypass, forged evidence, and production-operation requests.

executionDecision.reason: User explicitly authorized the sequential Trust Layer execution plan; high-risk runtime operations remain fail-closed.

preReviewWarnings: Critical-domain and governance-bypass paths must remain machine-readable and fail closed.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Authentication, authorization, payment, personal data, secrets, and production-release intent are detected deterministically.
Governance bypass, evidence forgery, and production-operation requests are rejected or routed to approval without becoming ready.
Safe local/test alternatives are named where a critical operation is blocked.
Critical-domain fixtures, bypass tests, forgery tests, and production-operation tests pass.
guidelines: Critical-domain decisions must be policy-first and fail closed.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: Preflight currently validates general readiness but does not classify critical-domain operations, governance bypass attempts, forged evidence, or production-operation requests with explicit fail-closed outcomes.
constraints: Policy-first detection must not infer approval from chat text.
High-risk requests require machine-readable approval evidence and remain not ready without valid structured evidence.
Offer safe local or test alternatives without executing external production operations.
intent.rationale: Critical-domain and governance guards make dangerous intent visible before implementation and prevent bypasses from reaching finish, PR, or release gates.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: critical authentication, authorization, payment, personal-data, secrets, or release request
governance bypass or production operation request
safe local/test alternative for blocked operation

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized the full sequential Trust Layer plan; runtime critical operations remain blocked unless separately evidenced.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
